name: Stregheria Italian
description: Stregheria, also known as Italian Witchcraft, is a modern Pagan religion and witchcraft tradition developed in the 1970s by Raven Grimassi. It draws inspiration from Italian folk traditions, pre-Christian beliefs, and historical accounts of witchcraft in Italy. Stregheria emphasizes a duotheistic cosmology, featuring a God and Goddess, often referred to as Dianus and Diana, representing the masculine and feminine principles of creation. Practices include spellcasting, ritual magic, herbalism, divination, and the veneration of ancestral spirits. Stregheria is characterized by its focus on personal empowerment, connection to nature, and the celebration of seasonal festivals. It differs from Wicca in its specific deities, pantheon of spirits, and emphasis on Italian cultural heritage. The tradition often incorporates elements of Italian folklore and mythology, providing a framework for spiritual growth and magical practice. Stregheria recognizes a variety of spirits and entities, including the Lares (household spirits), the Fata (faeries), and the various deities associated with nature and agriculture. The practice aims to bring balance and harmony to the individual and the community through magical workings and spiritual devotion. Stregheria places importance on family and community, and the role of the Strega (witch) as a healer, counselor, and protector of their community.
subtitle: Italian Witchcraft: Ancient Roots, Modern Practice

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Diana
keyMyths.description: Diana is born from the union of light and darkness, embodying both aspects of nature and magic. She is the queen of witches and the guardian of the wild.
keyMyths.source: Grimassi, Raven. Ways of the Strega

keyMyths.title: The Horned God Dianus
keyMyths.description: Dianus represents the masculine energy of nature, fertility, and the hunt. He is the consort of Diana and together they represent the divine couple.
keyMyths.source: Buckland, Raymond. Buckland's Complete Book of Witchcraft

sources.author: Raven Grimassi
sources.description: A comprehensive guide to the principles and practices of Stregheria.
sources.text: A comprehensive guide to the principles and practices of Stregheria.
